description: The Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) is a laboratory technique used to make millions of copies of a specific DNA segment.
CRISPR-Cas9 is a gene-editing process that enables precise modification of DNA within living cells.
Electrical cell stimulation is a technique in which controlled electric currents or fields are applied to cells to influence their behavior and function.

process_name: RNA-Seq Differential Gene Expression Analysis Workflow
description: RNA-Seq differential gene expression analysis is a computational workflow used to quantify and compare gene activity across biological conditions.

process_name: Bulk RNA-seq Library Preparation and Sequencing
description: RNA sequencing library preparation is the process used to turn RNA from biological samples into a form that can be read by sequencing machines.
Fatigue testing is a destructive mechanical testing process in which a metallic specimen is subjected to repeated cyclic loading until failure occurs.
Steam reforming is an industrial process used to produce hydrogen by reacting hydrocarbons with high-temperature steam.
Solvent casting for polymer composites is a fabrication process in which a polymer is dissolved in a suitable solvent and cast into a solid composite film.

process_name: Catalytic Pyrolysis of Mixed Plastic Waste
description: Catalytic pyrolysis is a thermal recycling process that breaks down plastic waste into valuable fuels and chemicals using heat and catalysts.
Metal-organic cages are synthesized through coordination-driven self-assembly of metal ions and organic ligands.
Dynamic Mechanical Analysis is used to study how a material responds to time-varying mechanical forces.
Iterative tomographic image reconstruction is a digital guess-and-check method used to create internal 2D or 3D images from raw X-ray data.
Multi-electrode array recordings are used to measure the electrical activity of cells using a grid of microelectrodes.
Neutrino event reconstruction transforms detector signals into estimates of particle interactions and physical quantities.
The Stroop task is a cognitive experiment used to measure how the brain processes conflicting information.
The Torrance Tests of Creative Thinking are standardized psychological assessments designed to evaluate creative thinking abilities.
